text: The WannaCry ransomware exploited the EternalBlue vulnerability (CVE-2017-0144) in Windows SMB protocol. The attack affected over 200,000 computers in 150 countries, with Lazarus Group identified as the likely threat actor.

A critical buffer overflow vulnerability (CVE-2018-4878) has been discovered in Adobe Flash Player. Users are advised to update to version 28.0.0.161 or later immediately. The vulnerability is being actively exploited in the wild.

Analysis of the Emotet trojan reveals it uses phishing emails with malicious Word documents as the initial infection vector. The malware communicates with command-and-control servers at 185.94.111.1 and uses Mimikatz for credential harvesting.

Researchers at FireEye discovered a new APT campaign attributed to APT28 targeting European government agencies. The attackers used spear-phishing emails containing a modified version of the X-Agent backdoor.
The Equifax data breach exposed personal information of 147 million consumers. The breach exploited an unpatched Apache Struts vulnerability (CVE-2017-5638) in the company's web application.

Organizations should apply the latest security patches for Microsoft Exchange Server to address ProxyLogon vulnerabilities (CVE-2021-26855). Hafnium, a state-sponsored group, has been exploiting these flaws since January.

The TrickBot banking trojan has evolved to include ransomware capabilities through its partnership with Ryuk ransomware operators. Infected systems communicate via domain generation algorithms to evade detection.

A supply chain attack compromised the SolarWinds Orion software update mechanism. The SUNBURST backdoor was distributed to approximately 18,000 customers, with Cozy Bear suspected as the responsible threat actor.
Security researchers recommend using tools like Wireshark and YARA rules to detect indicators of compromise associated with the new variant of Cobalt Strike beacon found in recent campaigns.
The Colonial Pipeline ransomware attack by DarkSide forced the shutdown of the largest fuel pipeline in the United States, highlighting critical infrastructure vulnerabilities in the energy sector.
